definition: Craving directed toward ending, annihilating, or escaping a mode of becoming.
notes: The project prefers craving for non-becoming because it stays closest to the doctrinal contrast with bhavataṇhā. Craving for annihilation is acceptable in explanatory prose when the force of elimination needs to be made more explicit.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose emphasizing the annihilating impulse

example_phrases.notes: One of the classic threefold forms of craving.
